texas_v10_rule_basis: Preserve noindex until all critical families are verified
District-grade routing must use direct local or district-owned evidence, not generic or statewide fallback
Legacy/inventory-only evidence does not satisfy California-grade completeness

final_blockers.evidence: Reviewed 2026-06-25 one bounded official New Mexico education directory pass beyond the previously failing PED hosts, then rechecked the live PED-managed SharePoint directory surfaces directly. The public school-directory host `https://webed.ped.state.nm.us/sites/schooldirectory/SitePages/Home.aspx` is live and returns HTTP 200. Its public navigation now proves more than the prior packet captured: the visible `NM Schools` list at `/Lists/2017 NM Schools/AllItems.aspx` is backed by a public SharePoint REST list with GUID `ed760a23-c290-4b26-8fec-4f94210cf7c3`, `ItemCount: 935`, and live `items` plus `RenderListDataAsStream` responses. But that live list schema still exposes only district and location columns such as `District Name`, `District Code`, `District Type`, `Location Name`, `Location Address`, `Location City`, `State`, `Zip`, `School Level`, `Location Type`, `Location Status`, and `Location Phone Number`, with no county field. The official public downloads `NM Schools.xlsx`, `Superintendents.xlsx`, and `REC Directors.xlsx` also return HTTP 200 from the same host, and they still carry no county field or REC county-service-area field. The public `RECHome.aspx` page is also live and stronger than a dead-host assumption, but it only groups districts under REC headings such as `REC 1 - Farmington, Aztec, Bloomfield, Central, Gallup-McKinley, Zuni` and similar district clusters; it still exposes no county labels or REC service-area contract. The legacy `education.new-mexico.gov` host family remains DNS-dead, and the current `webnew.ped.state.nm.us/bureaus/special-education/` host still does not itself supply county-grade local routing. New Mexico education therefore remains blocked on an official county-to-district or county-to-REC crosswalk, not on total absence of a public PED directory lane.

Reviewed 2026-06-22 the exact official New Mexico DVR root https://www.dvr.nm.gov/, which returned HTTP 401 Unauthorized in bounded fetches. The old packet still lacks any reviewed alternate first-party VR or Pre-ETS page on disk, so the family is no longer just missing-inventory; it is blocked on the official DVR lane requiring browser-assisted or alternate official-root review.
